checks.finding: Terms, tools, and states are concretely defined: 5 tool names with explicit field lists (REQ-002), error code enum (REQ-004/REQ-006), truncation size (256 KiB, REQ-008), and the previously ambiguous 401/403 mapping is now fully disambiguated in REQ-006 (401 always auth-missing; 403 rate-limited only with x-ratelimit-remaining:0 or retry-after; headerless 403 upstream-error; path-denied reserved for local input guard only). Remaining open items (OQ-001 owner/repo resolution priority, OQ-004 token env var name) are explicitly flagged as open questions with owners and resolution paths rather than left as silent ambiguity, so two implementers would not diverge without a flagged decision point.

REQ-006 and AC-010 now state an identical, mutually exclusive error-mapping table (401->auth-missing always; 403 with indicator->rate-limited; 403 without indicator->upstream-error; 404->not-found; 429->rate-limited; 5xx/network->upstream-error), resolving the round-1 401/403 disjunction. path-denied is explicitly scoped to REQ-007/AC-012 local input-guard use only and is never applied to upstream 403, removing the prior overlap. No other conflicts found between Goals, Non-goals, Requirements, and Acceptance Criteria (e.g., write-exclusion in Non-goals is consistently reinforced by REQ-003/AC-006/AC-007 with no contradicting requirement).

Edge Cases section and AC-008 through AC-012 cover: missing token (auth-missing), unresolvable owner/repo (invalid-input), not-found run/job ids, rate limit via both 429 and indicator-bearing 403, headerless 403 (upstream-error) now explicitly required to be tested per AC-010, log truncation boundary (AC-004), expired artifacts (non-error, AC-005), 5xx/network failure (upstream-error), and empty result set returning ok:true with empty array. This is a materially complete set of negative/boundary/failure paths implied by the requirements.

Assumptions section states target scope (github.com REST v3, read-only fine-grained PAT, three-OS installer support) as accepted premises, and OQ-001/OQ-002/OQ-003/OQ-004 each carry Owner, Blocks Implementation status, and Resolution Path, explicitly marking product decisions needed before final contract lock rather than leaving them as unstated assumptions baked into requirements text.

The write-exclusion boundary is explicit and testable: Non-goals lists all excluded write operations, REQ-003/AC-006/AC-007 require static/unit tests enforcing GET-only HTTP methods and absence of write-inducing input fields, and the Assumptions section states this boundary is recorded as ADR-0006 'and is subject to human approval.' Security Boundaries table (B1-B3) further specifies auth/trust boundaries. This gives a concrete, verifiable approval/governance boundary rather than an aspirational statement.

Requirements and acceptance tests give design/implementation-policy review sufficient concrete detail (tool contracts, error enum incl. resolved 401/403 mapping, size limits, static-check obligations, installer parity requirements) without requiring invention of missing product behavior. The remaining product-decision points (OQ-001, OQ-004) are correctly scoped as pre-design/pre-implementation blockers rather than gaps silently left for the design reviewer to resolve unilaterally.
